title: grants — model traits
description: Trait contract this module owns (HasResourceGrants, IsGrantable, BelongsToGrant) and consumes from foundation / tenancy / activity / framework. HasResourceGrants is the primary extension point — every domain aggregate that can be a grant target composes it; IsGrantable is the paired marker for policy-authoring introspection.

owned.description: Composed on any resource model that can be the TARGET of grants (Invoice, Athlete, Team, Document, ...). Adds a polymorphic `grants()` MorphMany relation + fluent helpers to enumerate active grants + issue new ones. Requires the composing model to register its `resource_type` key with GrantableRegistry in the owner module's service provider boot.

relations.name: grants
relations.description: All grants targeting this row, regardless of state (active + terminal).

relations.name: activeGrants
relations.description: Only currently-effective grants (revoked_at IS NULL AND (expires_at IS NULL OR expires_at > now())).

relations.name: activeDenies
relations.description: Active deny grants only — surfaces the inviolable blocks that will short-circuit any check.

methods.name: grantTo
methods.description: Create + persist an AccessGrant with this row as the resource. Params: `decision` (default `allow`), `expires_at`, `reason` (required, min 20 chars), `source` (default `manual`), `source_reference`, `metadata`. Dispatches GrantIssued after commit. Enforces cross-tenant refusal + grantor-privilege check + permission-catalogue validation at the observer layer.

methods.name: revokeGrant
methods.description: Transition a grant on this resource to `revoked`. Refused when the grant is already terminal.

methods.name: grantsFor
methods.description: Enumerate active grants for (this resource, given subject). Optional decision filter.

methods.name: canBeGrantedBy
methods.description: Preflight check: does the grantor hold all the permissions being granted? Consulted by the observer's privilege-escalation guard before insert.

notAppliedBy: tenancy::Tenant — tenants aren't grant targets; use RBAC roles instead
application::Application — applications don't accept grants; grants exist strictly inside a tenant
user::User — subjects RECEIVE grants; they aren't targets of them (though there could be niche 'grant view access on this user's profile' cases; deferred)

owned.description: Marker trait for policy authors. Signals 'this model participates in the grant overlay'. Composing IsGrantable + HasResourceGrants gives full participation; composing only IsGrantable (without HasResourceGrants) is a bug caught at boot by the GrantableRegistry integrity check.

methods.name: grantResourceType
methods.description: Static accessor returning the `resource_type` key registered with GrantableRegistry. Defaults to the class basename in snake_case; override to customize.

owned.description: Composed on models CREATED from a grant. Adds a nullable `grant_id` FK + BelongsTo relation for provenance / traceability. Primary consumer: rows that need to trace their existence back to the grant that authorised them (e.g. an audit row for 'Bob accessed invoice #123 because grant agr_...' — recorded by the activity/laravel-activitylog subscriber). Optional — most modules don't need it.
appliedBy: activity::ActivityLog rows for grant-authorised actions (via subject_type='activity_log' + grant_id set at write time by the activity subscriber)

consumed.description: Applies the tenant global scope + auto-fills tenant_id on save. Registered FIRST in the composition chain so subsequent traits observe tenant context in their booted() hooks.
Free-form metadata JSONB column + fluent accessors. Used for csv_row_index (bulk), approver_notes (approval), delegation_scope (delegation).
created_by / updated_by / deleted_by. Applied to AccessGrant so compliance can answer 'who created / updated / soft-deleted this grant' without joining audits.
Emits activity_log rows on every create / update / delete. Consumed by the tenant's activity feed.
Emits per-column diff audit rows via owen-it/laravel-auditing. Consumed by compliance for SOC 2 CC6.3 evidence.
Query-string filter support for the list endpoints (filter[state], filter[decision], filter[resource_type], etc.).
Full-text search over reason + revoked_reason for admin dashboards.
Soft-delete-then-hard-delete via the retention pipeline. AccessGrant is high-volume for busy tenants; retention windows are tight (90 days for revoked/expired).

compositionExample.note: The AccessGrant model — canonical composition example. Note BelongsToTenant is FIRST so subsequent traits observe the tenant context.
